markdown_projection.note: JSON contract is authority. No markdown projection is generated for WP-KERNEL-009 (prevents projection drift).

scope.scope: Prove unsaved/draft/promoted document state can recover after app crash or session compaction. This MT belongs to RichDocumentCore. Build the canonical structured document model backing the editor, Loom, UserManual, and projections.
constraints: Use the existing microtask_plan_draft execution surface only; do not introduce a separate MT execution surface or suite field.

Core Handshake behavior must be Handshake-native: no outside app, outside server, external daemon, Docker-only path, SQLite fallback, Redis/Valkey cache authority, or sidecar paper as proof or authority.

Allowed external runtime inputs are limited to operator-provided models, GGUF/model files, tensors/safetensors, and CUI portable artifacts where the owning WP explicitly gates them; Tiptap and Monaco are allowed only as bundled/lockfile-governed product libraries integrated into Handshake.

PostgreSQL plus EventLedger is canonical for durable state, receipts, indexing evidence, runtime proof, and validation; generated markdown, wiki pages, HTML, context bundles, debug reports, and UI projections are projections only.
Every implementation handoff must be restartable by a no-context local model using only the WP/MT contract, current Master Spec bundle, product code, tests, and receipts.
Rich documents are PostgreSQL/EventLedger/CRDT-backed structured block documents, not Markdown vault authority.
All rich document state must preserve Raw/Derived/Display separation and stable entity IDs.
scope.expected_behavior: DocumentCrashRecovery leaves a concrete, restartable implementation or activation artifact with PostgreSQL/EventLedger authority, Master Spec anchors, UserManual impact, and validator-readable evidence.
acceptance_criteria: DocumentCrashRecovery is implemented or specified with enough detail for a no-context local model to continue from the MT alone.
The MT names exact product/governance files, state tables or APIs, constraints, failure modes, and verification evidence.
The MT rejects sidecar research-paper authority and outside-runtime proof; accepted research ideas are restated as Handshake-native requirements.
The MT handoff names HBR rows proven, blocked, or not applicable with concrete reasons.
verification: Parse the WP/MT contract and confirm this MT keeps all required fields populated.
Run the smallest relevant product, governance, or fixture test available for the touched surface; if not implemented yet, define the exact future test and negative case.
For UI/editor/UserManual surfaces, use visual debugging or declare why no GUI surface exists for this MT.
Confirm no SQLite, Docker-default, external-app, external-daemon, sidecar-paper, or mock-only proof satisfies the MT.
handoff_requirements: List files changed or intentionally left untouched.
List commands/tests run and exact pass/fail/blocker status.
List EventLedger/PostgreSQL evidence, receipt IDs, fixtures, visual-debug artifacts, and repair queues produced or expected.
List remaining risks, compatibility shims, deprecated wording, and next MTs unblocked.

evidence: Built+verified (commits b506d832 backend core + 935e0482 manual + bf0c62a5 integration tests + dd3056f5 frontend); RichDocumentCore: 6 real-PG integration + 27 unit backend tests + 8 vitest (app suite 349/349). 18 block kinds, RawDerivedDisplay (CX-100), stable KBL- ids, save/load API w/ receipts, projections (negative proof: projection-delete leaves authority byte-identical), embeds (absolute paths DB-CHECK rejected), backlinks (stable KDLNK- relationship_id), history, permissions, crash recovery; migrations 0280-0282; RichDocumentView.tsx wired to real authority with stable selectors for MT-161+ GUI groups. UserManual coverage added (1 group + 10 cmd refs).

evidence.finding: Crash-recovery proof covered only promoted state; unsaved/draft (write-box) recovery untested
evidence.fix: Added draft write-box recovery proof: draft-labeled doc + WIP v2 save reconstructs fully over a fresh PG connection (label/content/sha/history/tree) and promotes post-recovery; a crash never silently promotes

findings.title: Crash-recovery proof covers only promoted (committed) state, not the unsaved/draft half of the contract
findings.evidence: crash_recovery_reconstructs_document_from_postgres (knowledge_document_model_tests.rs:454-499) creates+saves (committed) then reconnects PG and re-reads - this only proves committed rows survive (trivially durable). The contract scope is unsaved/draft/promoted recover after crash or session compaction; the draft authority_label path and CRDT write-box draft recovery are not exercised.
findings.fix: Add a test that lands an unsaved/draft (write-box / draft-label) document state and proves it recovers after a fresh connection / simulated compaction. Reconcile with any build-order descope note for MT-159.

claim_mismatches.claim: Prove unsaved/draft/promoted document state can recover
claim_mismatches.reality: only promoted state is proven recoverable; unsaved/draft recovery has no covering test

missing_tests: test: draft/unsaved document state recovers after crash (the non-trivial half of MT-159)

wp_validator_review_v2.review_method: Integration Validator multi-agent workflow (wf_0ac25815-485 + wf_98386044-70a): independent per-MT reviewer + adversarial confirm on FAIL. No ACP. Governance/repo paperwork non-blocking per operator directive (2026-06-18). Bar = good code vs MT contract + WP packet + current Master Spec v02.194, plus the WP_VALIDATOR Spec-Realism Gate.
wp_validator_review_v2.summary: MT-159 requires proving unsaved/draft/promoted rich-document state recovers after app crash or session compaction against PostgreSQL/EventLedger authority. The code delivers it: a real integration test reconstructs a draft (write-box) document — label, WIP content, sha, full version history, block tree — over a fresh PostgreSQL connection, proves a crash never silently promotes, and continues the lifecycle (promotion) post-recovery, all backed by genuine SQL implementations and the Handshake-managed PG harness (no SQLite/mock).
findings: Draft/write-box crash-recovery proof is real and complete: tests/knowledge_document_model_tests.rs:515-583 (crash_recovery_reconstructs_draft_writebox_state_from_postgres) creates a draft-labeled doc, saves a v2 WIP edit still in draft, then reconnects via PostgresDatabase::connect(&pg.schema_url,5) (a genuine fresh-process recovery) and asserts authority_label=='draft' (never silently promoted), doc_version==2, content_json==wip, content_sha256 match, 2-version history intact, and block-tree reconstruction; then promotes post-recovery — directly satisfying the unsaved/draft half the adversarial-v2 MEDIUM flagged as missing.

The prior adversarial-v2 finding (knowledge_document_model_tests.rs original crash_recovery test covered only promoted/committed state, lines ~454-499) is genuinely remediated by the new draft test, not just re-labeled. lifecycle.evidence and adversarial_review_v2.findings[0].status=='FIXED' (commit b8093659) match the code on disk.

Backing implementations are real PostgreSQL SQL, not stubs: save_knowledge_rich_document_version (storage/knowledge.rs:5117-5207) uses optimistic-concurrency UPDATE + append-only version INSERT in a tx; set_knowledge_rich_document_authority_label (5373-5398) is a CHECK-constrained UPDATE; get/list functions are real SELECTs. Migration 0280_knowledge_document_identity.sql:37-46 adds authority_label NOT NULL with a closed-vocabulary CHECK ('draft','promoted','archived').

Sub-rule 2 PASS: the PG harness (tests/knowledge_pg_support.rs:1-65) resolves to POSTGRES_TEST_URL/DATABASE_URL or the Handshake-managed no-Docker cluster, fresh isolated schema per test with full migration chain, and explicitly NO SQLite/in-memory/mock fallback (returns None -> SKIP only when PG binaries truly absent). Durable-authority claim is proven against real PostgreSQL.

Sub-rule 1 PASS: grep over storage/knowledge.rs, the test file, and src/knowledge_document/ found zero todo!()/unimplemented!()/unreachable!()/'deferred to follow-on'/'pending MT-'/*Unavailable escapes on the proof path.

Spec alignment: current Master Spec v02.194 02-system-architecture.md:2680-2692 mandates CRDT/write-box pre-promotion draft state, that drafts are not authority until promotion commits, and recovery from durable product rows alone without chat history — exactly what the test exercises.
Aside (non-blocking, expected): packet/MT cite v02.192 spec anchors while current is v02.194; same section numbers resolve. This is paperwork drift, not a code defect.
evidence_checked: git show b8093659 (MT-159 draft crash-recovery commit)

minimum_controls: No placeholders, mocks, scaffolding, or deferred-live escapes; runtime proof against Handshake-managed PostgreSQL/EventLedger where the contract names durable state.
